info.title: MCP tool validation and URL parsing fixes

parts.text:
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@internal/agent/agent.go` around lines 53 - 55, The Load() validation
currently enforces cfg.Tools against toolname.ValidNames() before MCP discovery,
causing valid MCP-provided tools to be rejected; update Load() (or its
validation helper) to either (A) relax/defer validation by skipping validation
of any tool name that matches an MCP-discovered tool name (query
MCPServers/MCPServerConfig discovery first and merge discovered tool names, then
validate only unknown names against toolname.ValidNames()), or (B) explicitly
treat Tools as "built-ins only" by renaming/scoping the field and validating
only against toolname.ValidNames(); reference the Tools field,
MCPServers/MCPServerConfig, the Load() function, and toolname.ValidNames() when
implementing the chosen fix. Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@internal/agent/agent.go` around lines 98 - 102, The config validation
currently only checks that server.URL is non-empty; instead parse and validate
the URL shape (use net/url.Parse or url.ParseRequestURI) for server.URL and
ensure it contains a valid scheme (http or https) and host before proceeding to
the Transport checks; if parsing fails return a clear error like
"mcp_servers[%d].url is invalid: %v" referencing server.URL, and keep the
existing transport check (server.Transport) after the URL validation so
malformed URLs fail fast and with actionable messages. Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@internal/mcpclient/mcpclient.go` around lines 85 - 90, The request spotlights
that CallTool currently forwards all arguments as strings (producing e.g. "3"
instead of 3) because schemas parsed in ListTools aren't cached; fix by caching
tool schemas on the Client (add a field like Client.toolSchemas
map[string]Schema populated in ListTools when calling convertTool) and perform
schema-aware coercion in Client.CallTool before invoking session.CallTool: look
up the stored schema for call.Name, coerce each entry in the args map to the
declared type (integer/number/boolean/string) using the same conversion logic as
convertTool, and then pass the coerced args into mcp.CallToolParams;
alternatively, if you prefer deferring coercion, ensure the code path that has
schema access (e.g., wherever convertTool is called) performs the coercion and
hands CallTool a typed args map rather than raw strings. Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@internal/mcpclient/router.go` around lines 11 - 17, Router.Close only
iterates r.tools which omits clients that registered zero routed tools, leaking
MCP sessions; modify registration logic so every Client is recorded for shutdown
regardless of tool filtering (e.g., add the Client to r.tools or a new r.clients
map at the start of Register or maintain a separate allClients set keyed by
client ID) and ensure Deregister/Close iterate that complete collection to call
Client.Close/cleanup; update Register, Close (and any Deregister/remove paths)
to use the chosen collection so clients that reported no routed tools are still
closed.
I'll start by examining the current code to verify each finding against the actual implementation. Let me explore all the relevant files in parallel.

state.output:
<path>/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/agent/agent.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package agent
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"errors"
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"os"
7: 	"path/filepath"
8: 	"strings"
9: 
10: 	"github.com/BurntSushi/toml"
11: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/toolname"
12: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/xdg"
13: )
14: 
15: // MemoryConfig holds memory sub-configuration for an agent.
16: type MemoryConfig struct {
17: 	Enabled    bool   `toml:"enabled"`
18: 	Path       string `toml:"path"`
19: 	LastN      int    `toml:"last_n"`
20: 	MaxEntries int    `toml:"max_entries"`
21: }
22: 
23: // ParamsConfig holds model parameter overrides for an agent.
24: type ParamsConfig struct {
25: 	Temperature float64 `toml:"temperature"`
26: 	MaxTokens   int     `toml:"max_tokens"`
27: }
28: 
29: // SubAgentsConfig holds sub-agent execution configuration for an agent.
30: type SubAgentsConfig struct {
31: 	MaxDepth int   `toml:"max_depth"`
32: 	Parallel *bool `toml:"parallel"`
33: 	Timeout  int   `toml:"timeout"`
34: }
35: 
36: // MCPServerConfig holds Model Context Protocol server configuration.
37: type MCPServerConfig struct {
38: 	Name      string            `toml:"name"`
39: 	URL       string            `toml:"url"`
40: 	Transport string            `toml:"transport"`
41: 	Headers   map[string]string `toml:"headers"`
42: }
43: 
44: // AgentConfig represents a parsed agent TOML configuration file.
45: type AgentConfig struct {
46: 	Name          string            `toml:"name"`
47: 	Description   string            `toml:"description"`
48: 	Model         string            `toml:"model"`
49: 	SystemPrompt  string            `toml:"system_prompt"`
50: 	Skill         string            `toml:"skill"`
51: 	Files         []string          `toml:"files"`
52: 	Workdir       string            `toml:"workdir"`
53: 	Tools         []string          `toml:"tools"`
54: 	MCPServers    []MCPServerConfig `toml:"mcp_servers"`
55: 	SubAgents     []string          `toml:"sub_agents"`
56: 	SubAgentsConf SubAgentsConfig   `toml:"sub_agents_config"`
57: 	Memory        MemoryConfig      `toml:"memory"`
58: 	Params        ParamsConfig      `toml:"params"`
59: }
60: 
61: // Validate checks that required fields are present in the agent configuration.
62: // It checks name first (fail-fast): if name is missing, it returns that error
63: // without checking model.
64: func Validate(cfg *AgentConfig) error {
65: 	if strings.TrimSpace(cfg.Name) == "" {
66: 		return errors.New("agent config missing required field: name")
67: 	}
68: 	if strings.TrimSpace(cfg.Model) == "" {
69: 		return errors.New("agent config missing required field: model")
70: 	}
71: 	if cfg.SubAgentsConf.MaxDepth < 0 {
72: 		return errors.New("sub_agents_config.max_depth must be non-negative")
73: 	}
74: 	if cfg.SubAgentsConf.MaxDepth > 5 {
75: 		return errors.New("sub_agents_config.max_depth cannot exceed 5")
76: 	}
77: 	if cfg.SubAgentsConf.Timeout < 0 {
78: 		return errors.New("sub_agents_config.timeout must be non-negative")
79: 	}
80: 	if cfg.Memory.LastN < 0 {
81: 		return errors.New("memory.last_n must be non-negative")
82: 	}
83: 	if cfg.Memory.MaxEntries < 0 {
84: 		return errors.New("memory.max_entries must be non-negative")
85: 	}
86: 	validTools := toolname.ValidNames()
87: 	for _, name := range cfg.Tools {
88: 		if !validTools[name] {
89: 			return fmt.Errorf("unknown tool %q in tools config", name)
90: 		}
91: 	}
92: 
93: 	seenMCPNames := make(map[string]struct{}, len(cfg.MCPServers))
94: 	for i, server := range cfg.MCPServers {
95: 		if strings.TrimSpace(server.Name) == "" {
96: 			return fmt.Errorf("mcp_servers[%d].name is required", i)
97: 		}
98: 		if strings.TrimSpace(server.URL) == "" {
99: 			return fmt.Errorf("mcp_servers[%d].url is required", i)
100: 		}
101: 		if server.Transport != "sse" && server.Transport != "streamable-http" {
102: 			return fmt.Errorf("mcp_servers[%d].transport must be one of: sse, streamable-http", i)
103: 		}
104: 		if _, exists := seenMCPNames[server.Name]; exists {
105: 			return fmt.Errorf("mcp_servers names must be unique: %q", server.Name)
106: 		}
107: 		seenMCPNames[server.Name] = struct{}{}
108: 	}
109: 	return nil
110: }
111: 
112: // Load reads and parses an agent TOML configuration file by name.
113: // The name parameter is the agent name without the .toml extension.
114: func Load(name string) (*AgentConfig, error) {
115: 	configDir, err := xdg.GetConfigDir()
116: 	if err != nil {
117: 		return nil, err
118: 	}
119: 
120: 	path := filepath.Join(configDir, "agents", name+".toml")
121: 
122: 	if _, err := os.Stat(path); os.IsNotExist(err) {
123: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent config not found: %s", name)
124: 	}
125: 
126: 	data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
127: 	if err != nil {
128: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read agent config %q: %w", name, err)
129: 	}
130: 
131: 	var cfg AgentConfig
132: 	if _, err := toml.Decode(string(data), &cfg); err != nil {
133: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse agent config %q: %w", name, err)
134: 	}
135: 
136: 	if err := Validate(&cfg); err != nil {
137: 		return nil, err
138: 	}
139: 
140: 	return &cfg, nil
141: }
142: 
143: // List returns all valid agent configurations from the agents directory.
144: // Invalid files are silently skipped. If the agents directory does not exist,
145: // an empty slice is returned.
146: func List() ([]AgentConfig, error) {
147: 	configDir, err := xdg.GetConfigDir()
148: 	if err != nil {
149: 		return nil, err
150: 	}
151: 
152: 	agentsDir := filepath.Join(configDir, "agents")
153: 
154: 	entries, err := os.ReadDir(agentsDir)
155: 	if err != nil {
156: 		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
157: 			return []AgentConfig{}, nil
158: 		}
159: 		return nil, err
160: 	}
161: 
162: 	var agents []AgentConfig
163: 	for _, entry := range entries {
164: 		if entry.IsDir() {
165: 			continue
166: 		}
167: 		if !strings.HasSuffix(entry.Name(), ".toml") {
168: 			continue
169: 		}
170: 		name := strings.TrimSuffix(entry.Name(), ".toml")
171: 		cfg, err := Load(name)
172: 		if err != nil {
173: 			continue // skip invalid files
174: 		}
175: 		agents = append(agents, *cfg)
176: 	}
177: 
178: 	return agents, nil
179: }
180: 
181: // Scaffold returns a TOML template string for a new agent configuration.
182: // The name argument is interpolated into the template.
183: func Scaffold(name string) (string, error) {
184: 	tmpl := `name = "` + name + `"
185: description = ""
186: 
187: # Full provider/model per models.dev
188: model = "provider/model-name"
189: 
190: # Agent persona (optional)
191: # system_prompt = ""
192: 
193: # Default skill (optional, can be overridden with --skill flag)
194: # skill = ""
195: 
196: # Context files - glob patterns resolved from workdir or cwd (optional)
197: # files = []
198: 
199: # Working directory (optional)
200: # workdir = ""
201: 
202: # Tools this agent can use (optional)
203: # Valid: list_directory, read_file, write_file, edit_file, run_command, url_fetch, web_search
204: # tools = []
205: 
206: # Sub-agents this agent can invoke (optional)
207: # sub_agents = []
208: 
209: # [sub_agents_config]
210: # max_depth = 3
211: # parallel = true
212: # timeout = 120
213: 
214: # MCP server connections (optional)
215: # [[mcp_servers]]
216: # name = "my-tools"
217: # url = "https://my-mcp-server.example.com/sse"
218: # transport = "sse"
219: # headers = { Authorization = "Bearer ${MY_TOKEN}" }
220: 
221: # [memory]
222: # enabled = false
223: # path = ""
224: # last_n = 10
225: # max_entries = 100
226: 
227: # [params]
228: # temperature = 0.3
229: # max_tokens = 4096
230: `
231: 	return tmpl, nil
232: }
233: 
234: // tomlDecode is a package-level wrapper for toml.Decode, used by tests.
235: var tomlDecode = toml.Decode

(End of file - total 235 lines)
</content>

package agent

import (
	"errors"
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"

	"github.com/BurntSushi/toml"
	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/toolname"
	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/xdg"
)

// MemoryConfig holds memory sub-configuration for an agent.
type MemoryConfig struct {
	Enabled    bool   `toml:"enabled"`
	Path       string `toml:"path"`
	LastN      int    `toml:"last_n"`
	MaxEntries int    `toml:"max_entries"`

state.output:
<path>/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/mcpclient/mcpclient.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package mcpclient
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"net/http"
7: 	"strconv"
8: 	"strings"
9: 
10: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/agent"
11: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/envinterp"
12: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/provider"
13: 	"github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk/mcp"
14: )
15: 
16: // Client wraps an MCP session for one configured server.
17: type Client struct {
18: 	name        string
19: 	session     *mcp.ClientSession
20: 	closeFn     func() error
21: 	toolSchemas map[string]map[string]string // tool name -> param name -> type
22: }
23: 
24: // Connect initializes an MCP client session for one server config.
25: func Connect(ctx context.Context, cfg agent.MCPServerConfig) (*Client, error) {
26: 	headers, err := envinterp.ExpandHeaders(cfg.Headers)
27: 	if err != nil {
28: 		return nil, err
29: 	}
30: 
31: 	httpClient := &http.Client{Transport: &headerRoundTripper{base: http.DefaultTransport, headers: headers}}
32: 
33: 	var transport mcp.Transport
34: 	switch cfg.Transport {
35: 	case "sse":
36: 		transport = &mcp.SSEClientTransport{Endpoint: cfg.URL, HTTPClient: httpClient}
37: 	case "streamable-http":
38: 		transport = &mcp.StreamableClientTransport{Endpoint: cfg.URL, HTTPClient: httpClient}
39: 	default:
40: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unsupported MCP transport %q", cfg.Transport)
41: 	}
42: 
43: 	client := mcp.NewClient(&mcp.Implementation{Name: "axe", Version: "1.0.0"}, nil)
44: 	session, err := client.Connect(ctx, transport, nil)
45: 	if err != nil {
46: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("connect MCP server %q: %w", cfg.Name, err)
47: 	}
48: 
49: 	return &Client{name: cfg.Name, session: session}, nil
50: }
51: 
52: // Name returns the configured server name.
53: func (c *Client) Name() string {
54: 	return c.name
55: }
56: 
57: // Close closes the underlying MCP session.
58: func (c *Client) Close() error {
59: 	if c == nil {
60: 		return nil
61: 	}
62: 	if c.closeFn != nil {
63: 		return c.closeFn()
64: 	}
65: 	if c.session == nil {
66: 		return nil
67: 	}
68: 	return c.session.Close()
69: }
70: 
71: // ListTools returns MCP tools converted to provider tools.
72: func (c *Client) ListTools(ctx context.Context) ([]provider.Tool, error) {
73: 	res, err := c.session.ListTools(ctx, &mcp.ListToolsParams{})
74: 	if err != nil {
75: 		return nil, err
76: 	}
77: 
78: 	c.toolSchemas = make(map[string]map[string]string, len(res.Tools))
79: 	tools := make([]provider.Tool, 0, len(res.Tools))
80: 	for _, tool := range res.Tools {
81: 		pt := convertTool(tool)
82: 		tools = append(tools, pt)
83: 
84: 		paramTypes := make(map[string]string, len(pt.Parameters))
85: 		for name, param := range pt.Parameters {
86: 			paramTypes[name] = param.Type
87: 		}
88: 		c.toolSchemas[tool.Name] = paramTypes
89: 	}
90: 	return tools, nil
91: }
92: 
93: // CallTool calls an MCP tool and converts the response into provider format.
94: func (c *Client) CallTool(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall) (provider.ToolResult, error) {
95: 	args := make(map[string]any, len(call.Arguments))
96: 	schema := c.toolSchemas[call.Name] // nil-safe: index on nil map returns ""
97: 	for key, value := range call.Arguments {
98: 		args[key] = coerceArg(value, schema[key])
99: 	}
100: 
101: 	res, err := c.session.CallTool(ctx, &mcp.CallToolParams{Name: call.Name, Arguments: args})
102: 	if err != nil {
103: 		return provider.ToolResult{}, err
104: 	}
105: 
106: 	textParts := make([]string, 0, len(res.Content))
107: 	for _, content := range res.Content {
108: 		text, ok := content.(*mcp.TextContent)
109: 		if !ok {
110: 			continue
111: 		}
112: 		textParts = append(textParts, text.Text)
113: 	}
114: 
115: 	if len(textParts) == 0 {
116: 		return provider.ToolResult{CallID: call.ID, IsError: true, Content: "MCP tool returned no text content"}, nil
117: 	}
118: 
119: 	return provider.ToolResult{CallID: call.ID, IsError: res.IsError, Content: strings.Join(textParts, "\n")}, nil
120: }
121: 
122: // coerceArg converts a string argument value to the type declared in the schema.
123: // If conversion fails the original string is returned.
124: func coerceArg(value, typeName string) any {
125: 	switch typeName {
126: 	case "integer":
127: 		if v, err := strconv.ParseInt(value, 10, 64); err == nil {
128: 			return v
129: 		}
130: 	case "number":
131: 		if v, err := strconv.ParseFloat(value, 64); err == nil {
132: 			return v
133: 		}
134: 	case "boolean":
135: 		if v, err := strconv.ParseBool(value); err == nil {
136: 			return v
137: 		}
138: 	}
139: 	return value
140: }
141: 
142: func convertTool(tool *mcp.Tool) provider.Tool {
143: 	out := provider.Tool{
144: 		Name:        tool.Name,
145: 		Description: tool.Description,
146: 		Parameters:  map[string]provider.ToolParameter{},
147: 	}
148: 
149: 	schema, ok := tool.InputSchema.(map[string]any)
150: 	if !ok {
151: 		return out
152: 	}
153: 
154: 	required := map[string]bool{}
155: 	if reqList, ok := schema["required"].([]any); ok {
156: 		for _, item := range reqList {
157: 			if name, ok := item.(string); ok {
158: 				required[name] = true
159: 			}
160: 		}
161: 	} else if reqList, ok := schema["required"].([]string); ok {
162: 		for _, name := range reqList {
163: 			required[name] = true
164: 		}
165: 	}
166: 
167: 	properties, ok := schema["properties"].(map[string]any)
168: 	if !ok {
169: 		return out
170: 	}
171: 
172: 	for name, raw := range properties {
173: 		param := provider.ToolParameter{Type: "string", Required: required[name]}
174: 		property, ok := raw.(map[string]any)
175: 		if !ok {
176: 			param.Description = "Type unknown"
177: 			out.Parameters[name] = param
178: 			continue
179: 		}
180: 
181: 		desc, _ := property["description"].(string)
182: 		if typeName, ok := property["type"].(string); ok {
183: 			switch typeName {
184: 			case "string", "integer", "number", "boolean":
185: 				param.Type = typeName
186: 				param.Description = desc
187: 			default:
188: 				param.Description = prependTypeDescription(typeName, desc)
189: 			}
190: 		} else {
191: 			param.Description = prependTypeDescription("unknown", desc)
192: 		}
193: 
194: 		out.Parameters[name] = param
195: 	}
196: 
197: 	return out
198: }
199: 
200: func prependTypeDescription(typeName, desc string) string {
201: 	if desc == "" {
202: 		return fmt.Sprintf("Type %s", typeName)
203: 	}
204: 	return fmt.Sprintf("Type %s: %s", typeName, desc)
205: }
206: 
207: type headerRoundTripper struct {
208: 	base    http.RoundTripper
209: 	headers map[string]string
210: }
211: 
212: func (h *headerRoundTripper) RoundTrip(req *http.Request) (*http.Response, error) {
213: 	clone := req.Clone(req.Context())
214: 	for key, value := range h.headers {
215: 		clone.Header.Set(key, value)
216: 	}
217: 
218: 	base := h.base
219: 	if base == nil {
220: 		base = http.DefaultTransport
221: 	}
222: 	return base.RoundTrip(clone)
223: }

(End of file - total 223 lines)
</content>

import (

	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/agent"
	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/envinterp"

	"github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk/mcp"

state.output:
<path>/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/mcpclient/router.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package mcpclient
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"fmt"
6: 
7: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/provider"
8: )
9: 
10: // Router routes MCP tool calls to owning clients.
11: type Router struct {
12: 	tools   map[string]*Client
13: 	clients []*Client
14: }
15: 
16: // NewRouter returns a new empty MCP tool router.
17: func NewRouter() *Router {
18: 	return &Router{tools: map[string]*Client{}}
19: }
20: 
21: // Register adds tool routes for a client and returns tools not skipped.
22: func (r *Router) Register(client *Client, tools []provider.Tool, builtinNames map[string]bool) ([]provider.Tool, error) {
23: 	r.clients = append(r.clients, client)
24: 	filtered := make([]provider.Tool, 0, len(tools))
25: 	for _, tool := range tools {
26: 		if builtinNames[tool.Name] {
27: 			continue
28: 		}
29: 
30: 		if existing, ok := r.tools[tool.Name]; ok && existing != client {
31: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("MCP tool %q collision between servers %q and %q", tool.Name, existing.Name(), client.Name())
32: 		}
33: 
34: 		r.tools[tool.Name] = client
35: 		filtered = append(filtered, tool)
36: 	}
37: 
38: 	return filtered, nil
39: }
40: 
41: // Dispatch routes a tool call to the correct MCP client.
42: func (r *Router) Dispatch(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall) (provider.ToolResult, error) {
43: 	client, ok := r.tools[call.Name]
44: 	if !ok {
45: 		return provider.ToolResult{}, fmt.Errorf("unknown MCP tool %q", call.Name)
46: 	}
47: 	return client.CallTool(ctx, call)
48: }
49: 
50: // Has reports whether the router has a route for the given tool name.
51: func (r *Router) Has(name string) bool {
52: 	_, ok := r.tools[name]
53: 	return ok
54: }
55: 
56: // ServerName returns the MCP server name that owns a tool.
57: func (r *Router) ServerName(name string) (string, bool) {
58: 	client, ok := r.tools[name]
59: 	if !ok {
60: 		return "", false
61: 	}
62: 	return client.Name(), true
63: }
64: 
65: // Close closes each unique client once.
66: func (r *Router) Close() error {
67: 	closed := map[*Client]struct{}{}
68: 	var firstErr error
69: 
70: 	for _, client := range r.clients {
71: 		if _, seen := closed[client]; seen {
72: 			continue
73: 		}
74: 		closed[client] = struct{}{}
75: 		if err := client.Close(); err != nil && firstErr == nil {
76: 			firstErr = err
77: 		}
78: 	}
79: 
80: 	return firstErr
81: }

(End of file - total 81 lines)
</content>

<task_result>
Here are the full contents of all four files found:

---

## File 1: `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/toolname/toolname.go` (30 lines)

This file defines the `ValidNames()` function along with tool name constants. It exports a set of 7 valid tool names (`list_directory`, `read_file`, `write_file`, `edit_file`, `run_command`, `url_fetch`, `web_search`) as a `map[string]bool`. Notably, `call_agent` is defined as a constant but intentionally excluded from `ValidNames()` because it is controlled by the `sub_agents` config field.

---

## File 2: `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/agent/agent_test.go` (1150 lines)

This is a comprehensive test file covering:
- **Validate tests** (lines 10-78): Missing/empty name, missing/empty model, whitespace-only fields, valid config.
- **Load tests** (lines 80-313): Valid full config, minimal config, missing file, malformed TOML, missing name/model, whitespace name/model. Uses temp XDG config dirs.
- **List tests** (lines 315-412): Empty directory, no directory, multiple agents, skips invalid files, ignores non-TOML, ignores subdirectories.
- **SubAgentsConfig tests** (lines 414-531): Loading sub_agents_config fields, defaults, max_depth validation (too high, negative, valid).
- **Memory config tests** (lines 533-651): All memory fields, defaults, negative last_n/max_entries validation.
- **Scaffold tests** (lines 653-945): Scaffold output includes sub_agents_config, memory fields, name, model placeholder, valid TOML, tools comment, valid tool names listing, tools-before-sub_agents ordering.
- **Tools field tests** (lines 739-945): Load with/without/empty tools, validate valid/unknown/call_agent/empty-string tools.
- **MCP server tests** (lines 947-1150): Validate MCP server configs (missing name/URL, invalid transport, duplicate names, valid transports), TOML parsing of `[[mcp_servers]]`, scaffold includes MCP example.

---

## File 3: `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/mcpclient/mcpclient_test.go` (417 lines)

Tests the MCP client implementation:
- **Connect tests**: Streamable-HTTP success, invalid transport rejection, unreachable server error.
- **Header injection**: Verifies `${TOKEN}` env var expansion in headers, and error when env var is missing.
- **ListTools tests**: Returns tools from server, handles empty tools, complex schemas (array types coerced to string type with description prefix).
- **CallTool tests**: Success case, tool error propagation, no-text-content handling, multiple text content joined with newline.
- **Client.Name()**: Verifies the name accessor.
- **coerceArg tests**: Table-driven tests for integer, number, boolean, string, and fallback coercion.
- **Typed args integration**: Verifies arguments are coerced to correct types before being sent to the MCP server.

Uses helper functions `mustConnectTestClient`, `agentTestConfig`, `newMCPStreamableServer`, `newMCPComplexSchemaServer`, and `newMCPTypedArgsServer` which spin up real `httptest.Server` instances backed by the MCP SDK.

---

## File 4: `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/mcpclient/router_test.go` (185 lines)

Tests the MCP tool router:
- **Register and Dispatch**: Registers tools from a client, dispatches a call, verifies result.
- **Has for unknown tool**: Returns false for unregistered tools.
- **Dispatch unknown tool**: Returns error with "unknown MCP tool" message.
- **Register skips builtins**: Tools in the builtins map are filtered out and not registered.
- **MCP collision detection**: Two servers with overlapping tool names produce a collision error naming both servers.
- **Close tests**: Closes all clients, deduplicates shared clients (only closes once), closes zero-tool clients (clients where all tools were filtered as builtins), and returns the first error encountered during close.
</task_result>
state.title: Explore toolname and test files

parts.text:
Excellent. Now I have a clear picture of all four files and their test coverage. Let me verify each finding systematically.

---

## Verification of Each Finding

### Finding 1: Tool validation in `Validate()` rejects MCP-provided tools (agent.go lines 86-91)

**Claim:** `Load()` validation enforces `cfg.Tools` against `toolname.ValidNames()` before MCP discovery, causing valid MCP-provided tools to be rejected.

**Actual code (lines 86-91):**
```go
validTools := toolname.ValidNames()
for _, name := range cfg.Tools {
    if !validTools[name] {
        return fmt.Errorf("unknown tool %q in tools config", name)
    }
}
```

**Verdict: The finding is based on a misconception.** The `Tools` field (line 53) and `MCPServers` field (line 54) are **separate config fields**. `Tools` is a list of built-in tool names (like `read_file`, `write_file`, etc.), while `MCPServers` defines MCP server connections whose tools are discovered at runtime. A user would never put an MCP tool name in the `Tools` field — that's not how the config works. MCP tools are discovered dynamically via `Client.ListTools()` and routed via the `Router`. The validation of `Tools` against `toolname.ValidNames()` is **correct as-is** — it's validating built-in tool names only.

The Scaffold template (line 203) makes this clear: `# Valid: list_directory, read_file, write_file, edit_file, run_command, url_fetch, web_search`. **No fix needed.**

---

### Finding 2: URL validation for MCP servers (agent.go lines 98-102)

**Claim:** The config validation only checks that `server.URL` is non-empty; should parse/validate URL shape.

**Actual code (lines 98-102):**
```go
if strings.TrimSpace(server.URL) == "" {
    return fmt.Errorf("mcp_servers[%d].url is required", i)
}
if server.Transport != "sse" && server.Transport != "streamable-http" {
    return fmt.Errorf("mcp_servers[%d].transport must be one of: sse, streamable-http", i)
}
```

**Verdict: This is a legitimate improvement.** The URL is only checked for emptiness. A malformed URL like `"not-a-url"` or `"ftp://foo"` would pass validation but fail later at connection time with a less actionable error. Adding `net/url.ParseRequestURI` validation and scheme checks would fail fast with a clear message. **Fix warranted.**

---

### Finding 3: CallTool forwards all arguments as strings (mcpclient.go lines 85-90)

**Claim:** CallTool currently forwards all arguments as strings because schemas aren't cached; fix by caching tool schemas and performing schema-aware coercion.

**Actual code:** The `Client` already has:
- Line 21: `toolSchemas map[string]map[string]string` field
- Lines 78-88 in `ListTools()`: Populates `c.toolSchemas` from discovered tools
- Lines 95-99 in `CallTool()`: Uses `c.toolSchemas` to look up types and calls `coerceArg()`
- Lines 124-140: `coerceArg()` function that converts strings to `int64`, `float64`, or `bool`

**Verdict: This has already been fixed.** The code already caches tool schemas in `Client.toolSchemas`, looks them up in `CallTool`, and performs type coercion via `coerceArg()`. The test file even has `coerceArg` tests and a "typed args integration" test. **No fix needed.**

---

### Finding 4: Router.Close only iterates r.tools, leaking MCP sessions (router.go lines 11-17)

**Claim:** `Router.Close` only iterates `r.tools` which omits clients that registered zero routed tools, leaking MCP sessions.

**Actual code:**
- Line 13: `clients []*Client` field exists on Router
- Line 23 in `Register()`: `r.clients = append(r.clients, client)` — every client is recorded **before** any filtering
- Lines 66-80 in `Close()`: Iterates `r.clients` (not `r.tools`), deduplicates with a `closed` map

**Verdict: This has already been fixed.** The Router has a separate `clients` slice that captures every registered client regardless of whether any tools survived filtering. `Close()` iterates `r.clients`, not `r.tools`. The test file confirms this with a "closes zero-tool clients" test case. **No fix needed.**

---

## Summary

| # | Finding | Status | Action |
|---|---------|--------|--------|
| 1 | Tools validation rejects MCP tool names | **Not a real bug** — `Tools` and `MCPServers` are separate config concepts | No fix needed |
| 2 | URL validation is too weak for MCP servers | **Valid finding** — only checks emptiness, not URL shape/scheme | Fix warranted |
| 3 | CallTool forwards args as strings | **Already fixed** — `toolSchemas` cache + `coerceArg()` exist | No fix needed |
| 4 | Router.Close leaks zero-tool clients | **Already fixed** — `r.clients` slice + Close iterates it | No fix needed |

---

Only **Finding 2** (URL validation for MCP servers) warrants a code change. Here's my proposed plan:

todos.content: Finding 1: Tools vs MCP validation — verified no fix needed (separate config concepts)
Finding 2: Add URL shape validation (scheme + host) for mcp_servers[].url in Validate()
Finding 3: CallTool arg coercion — verified already fixed (toolSchemas + coerceArg)
Finding 4: Router.Close client leak — verified already fixed (r.clients slice)

Done. Here's the summary:

## Results

| # | Finding | Verdict | Action |
|---|---------|---------|--------|
| 1 | `Tools` validation rejects MCP tool names | **Not a bug** — `Tools` and `MCPServers` are separate config fields; `Tools` is strictly for built-ins | No change |
| 2 | URL validation too weak for MCP servers | **Valid** — only checked emptiness | **Fixed** |
| 3 | `CallTool` forwards args as strings | **Already fixed** — `toolSchemas` cache + `coerceArg()` already exist at `mcpclient.go:21,78-88,96-99,124-140` | No change |
| 4 | `Router.Close` leaks zero-tool clients | **Already fixed** — `r.clients` slice at `router.go:13,23` + `Close()` iterates it at `router.go:70` | No change |

### Changes made (Finding 2 only)

**`internal/agent/agent.go`** — Added `net/url` import and 9 lines of URL validation in `Validate()` after the emptiness check: parses with `url.ParseRequestURI`, rejects non-http/https schemes, rejects missing host. Errors are actionable: `"mcp_servers[%d].url is invalid: ..."`, `"...must use http or https scheme, got %q"`, `"...must include a host"`.

**`internal/agent/agent_test.go`** — Added 4 tests: `TestValidate_MCPServers_MalformedURL`, `TestValidate_MCPServers_InvalidScheme`, `TestValidate_MCPServers_MissingHost`, `TestValidate_MCPServers_ValidHTTPS`. All pass.
